role_title: Fraud and Disputes Member Experience Program Manager

level_signals: Minimum 2+ years of experience in process improvement, operations analysis, or process improvement
Role is contributor, not manager — acts as liaison and core contributor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum 2+ years of experience in process improvement, operations analysis, or process improvement, preferably within a transformational environment.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to gather, interpret, and analyze complex data sets to drive actionable insights.
Proficiency in process mapping, workflow analysis, root cause analysis, and process optimization techniques
Experience in the financial services industry
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively engage and influence stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
Highly motivated self-starter with a passion for driving continuous improvement and operational excellence.
Effectively network and build partnerships in a fast-paced environment
Ability to work in a very fast-paced, dynamic, and sometimes ambiguous environment

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum 2+ years of experience in process improvement, operations analysis, or process improvement, preferably within a transformational environment.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska (due to insurance coverage issues)

scope.surface: Fraud and Disputes operations — agent experience, fraud/dispute lifecycle, member-facing resolution processes
core_responsibilities: Drive implementation of solutions to fraud tools or policy changes (30%)
Review existing fraud and dispute workflows to pinpoint bottlenecks and inefficiencies; use data to highlight pain points (25%)
Assist in developing and proposing process updates and workflow redesigns; gain alignment from stakeholders (25%)
Help prepare cost-benefit analyses and justifications for prioritization (10%)
Manage intake process for agent feedback and maintain pipeline of potential opportunities; build relationships with frontline staff (10%)

green_flags: Clear breakdown of role responsibilities by time allocation (30/25/25/10/10%)
Explicit mission framing around member trust and empathy, not just efficiency
Relatively accessible seniority bar (2+ years) for an operational PM/program role
EEO commitment and accommodation process explicitly stated
San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance compliance noted
red_flags: Base pay range referenced but not disclosed in the JD body — candidate must visit a separate page
Location type is unclear — no city listed, no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite designation in the JD body
Remote work explicitly excluded from Hawaii and Alaska only — implies some remote eligibility but not confirmed
'Financial services industry' listed as a hard requirement with no equivalent experience caveat, which may screen out otherwise qualified ops candidates
Repeated phrase in JD ('process improvement, operations analysis, or process improvement') suggests minor editorial error, not substantive

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years minimum and role scope; not explicitly stated)
location.type (set to 'unclear'; no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite designation in JD body)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as true from Hawaii/Alaska exclusion implying other remote is allowed)

scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)

ambiguities: Location: JD does not specify city or work arrangement type (remote/hybrid/onsite). The only location signal is that remote from Hawaii or Alaska is excluded, implying remote elsewhere may be permissible, but this is not confirmed.

Financial services experience requirement: listed as a flat bullet under 'What You'll Need' without 'preferred' qualifier, treated as hard. However, it could be soft given the 'preferably' qualifier used for the transformational environment bullet nearby.
Visa sponsorship: not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
